title: exohebea lata var. longibracteata
intro: Exohebea lata var. longibracteata is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Exohebea lata var. longibracteata include the potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Exohebea lata var. longibracteata are often made by infusing the leaves or stems in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to treat various health issues.
